interactive.rationale: The concept now has mode and position controls, an Open cascade action, a Close all action, live status cards, a code readout, and visible popover state changes.

references.rationale: The page now cites the canonical ChromeStatus entry 5120638407409664 plus direct HTML popover, CSS anchor positioning, and WHATWG change links.

design_system.rationale: Inline styles route through --bg/--text/--accent variables, no raw hex, and button overrides pair background + color correctly.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature card and concept lede both describe the cascade-menu comparison across declarative invokers, showPopover({ source }), and the missing-source failure case.

spec_match.rationale: The page directly exercises popoverTargetElement, declarative popovertarget, showPopover({ source }), position-anchor:auto, and position-area for nested popovers.

edge_cases.rationale: The missing-source mode is a visible failure branch: showPopover() opens the child without an implicit anchor, and the live readout explains the broken parent/source relationship.

summary: The anchor-chain concept now demonstrates the Chrome 132 popover/source/implicit-anchor behavior with real controls, direct references, a live readout, and a visible missing-source failure branch.
